Package com.here.platform.location.core.graph.patch

Interface VirtualVertexCodec<V>

Type Parameters:
V - The vertex type of the graph.
All Known Subinterfaces:
VirtualIndexCodec<V,E>
public interface VirtualVertexCodec<V>
A codec that encodes and decodes virtual vertices within the concrete vertex type V of a patched graph.

Virtual vertices are synthetic graph elements introduced by a patch — they exist in the patched graph but not in the original. Each virtual vertex is identified by a zero-based VirtualVertexIndex.

Implementations pack these indices inside the existing type V (for example by using a reserved tile id), avoiding new type allocations while still allowing virtual vertices to be distinguished from original ones at runtime.

  • Method Summary

    Modifier and Type
    Method
    Description
    boolean
    isVirtualVertex(V v)
    Returns `true` if `v` is a virtual vertex, i.e.
    V
    virtualVertex(VirtualVertexIndex index)
    Creates the virtual vertex that corresponds to the given index.
    VirtualVertexIndex
    virtualVertexIndex(V v)
    Returns the zero-based index of a virtual vertex.

  • Method Details

    • isVirtualVertex

      boolean isVirtualVertex(V v)
      Returns `true` if `v` is a virtual vertex, i.e. a synthetic vertex introduced by a patch.

    • virtualVertex

      V virtualVertex(VirtualVertexIndex index)
      Creates the virtual vertex that corresponds to the given index.

      This is the inverse of virtualVertexIndex(V): virtualVertexIndex(virtualVertex(i)) == i.

      Parameters:
      index - Zero-based index into the virtual vertex space.

    • virtualVertexIndex

      VirtualVertexIndex virtualVertexIndex(V v)
      Returns the zero-based index of a virtual vertex.

      Parameters:
      v - A virtual vertex.
